title: andruris crinita
intro: Andruris crinita, also known as the hairy chervil or meadow chervil, is a flowering plant that belongs to the parsley family and is native to Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Andruris crinita include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Andruris crinita can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or as a tincture by mixing the plant extract with a carrier oil or alcohol.
